denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 04/25/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100728548          Group #: GRP-1092
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-116625
Claim #: CLM-2026-192877
Date of Service (requested): 04/05/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Harborview Clinical Partners

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination regarding prior authorization. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 04/07/2026 for Manipulation under anesthesia related to Frozen shoulder (M75.00). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Manipulation under anesthesia
Diagnosis (as submitted): Frozen shoulder (M75.00)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied:
Guideline applied: pediatric imaging criteria (ages 0–17) referenced for this request (incorrect demographic guideline). CDG-UM112 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM112 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Continuation of Manipulation under anesthesia requires an active prior authorization; the prior auth lapsed. A new authorization must be submitted.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with continuation of care lapsed. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director. Peer-to-peer review must be requested before a formal appeal is initiated for the same authorization decision.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100728548) and reference PA-2026-116625. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 27-year-old member has Frozen shoulder (M75.00). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Manipulation under anesthesia recommended as the next appropriate step. ROM flexion 90° after 5 months PT and intra-articular steroid. The prior-authorization packet included dated clinic notes, medication trials, and objective findings supporting medical necessity. The denial rationale does not reference those submissions. Treating clinicians document ongoing functional impairment and recommend proceeding without further delay when benefit-plan criteria have been met based on the record on file.
